g.text: Ch and Maj: Boolean Truth Tables (sha256.h)
x | y | z || Ch | if x then y else z
Ch = (x AND y) XOR (NOT x AND z)
x | y | z || Maj | majority of three bits
Maj = (x AND y) XOR (x AND z) XOR (y AND z)
Function | Formula | Linear? | Balanced? | Crypto Use
XOR(a,b) | a ⊕ b | YES | YES | Diffusion only — WEAK
AND(a,b) | a · b (GF2) | NO | NO | Not balanced — cannot use alone
Ch(x,y,z)| (x∧y)⊕(¬x∧z) | NO | YES | SHA-256 compression — CORRECT
Maj(x,y,z)|(x∧y)⊕(x∧z)⊕(y∧z)| NO | YES | SHA-256 compression — CORRECT
Proof: Ch(1,0,0) XOR Ch(0,1,0) ≠ Ch(1 XOR 0, 0 XOR 1, 0 XOR 0)
→ Linear cryptanalysis fails: no linear approx with bias ≫ 0
GF(2) addition | a ⊕ b (XOR) — linear map: f(a⊕b) = f(a)⊕f(b)
GF(2) multiply | a · b (AND) — NOT linear: creates cross-term ab
XOR-only system | Expressible as matrix · input over GF(2)
XOR-only attack | Gaussian elimination solves in O(n^3) — trivial
Ch contains AND | Cross-term x·y appears → cannot linearize
Maj contains AND | Cross-terms x·y, x·z, y·z → three-way coupling
Defense achieved | No linear approx with bias > 2^-16 for 64 rounds
Check | Inputs | Ch Output | Linear would give
Verdict | Ch(A XOR B) ≠ Ch(A) XOR Ch(B) | NONLINEAR confirmed
Formal | Ch not in span of {x,y,z,1} over GF(2)
Check | Inputs | Maj Output | Linear would give
Verdict | Maj(A XOR B) ≠ Maj(A) XOR Maj(B) | NONLINEAR confirmed
Formal | Maj degree 2 over GF(2) → three AND cross-terms
